title: Hobbies & Free Time
description: Practice talking about hobbies and free-time activities in Czech, describing what you do after work and on weekends.

data.explanation: koníček = hobby. It's a diminutive of kůň (horse) — literally 'little horse' — but in everyday Czech it means 'hobby' or 'pastime'. Jaké máš koníčky? = What hobbies do you have?

exercises.prompt: Which hobby word is a musical instrument?

data.question_en: Which of these hobby words is a musical instrument?
data.explanation: kytara = guitar. plavání = swimming, zahrada = garden/gardening, fotbal = football/soccer.

exercises.prompt: Complete using hrát na + instrument in accusative.

data.explanation: hrát na + accusative = to play (an instrument). já hraju/hraji = I play. kytara (guitar) → accusative kytaru. So: Hraju na kytaru = I play guitar.

exercises.prompt: Translate 'She plays the piano every day' to Czech.

data.source: She plays the piano every day
accepted_answers: Ona hraje na klavír každý den
data.grammar_note: hrát na + accusative: klavír (piano, masc. inanimate) → accusative klavír (same as nominative). každý den = every day. Time expression can go at the end or beginning.

exercises.answer_key: Ona hraje na klavír každý den

exercises.prompt: Complete using dívat se na + accusative.

data.explanation: dívat se na + accusative = to watch. já → dívám se. televize (TV, feminine) → accusative televizi. Rád se dívám na televizi = I like watching TV (male speaker).

data.question_en: What does 'Někdy' mean as a frequency adverb?
data.explanation: někdy = sometimes. Other frequency adverbs: vždycky = always, často = often, občas = occasionally, málokdy = rarely, nikdy = never.

exercises.prompt: Complete with the correct frequency adverb.

data.sentence: ___ (often) chodím do kina, ale ___ (never) do divadla.
data.explanation: Často = often. nikdy = never. Notice the word order: frequency adverb first, then the verb. 'Chodím do kina' = I go to the cinema. 'do divadla' = to the theatre.

exercises.prompt: Translate 'I sometimes play football with friends' to Czech.

data.source: I sometimes play football with friends
data.grammar_note: Někdy/Občas = sometimes. hrát fotbal = to play football (no 'na' — it's for instruments only). s kamarády = with friends (instrumental case).

exercises.prompt: Complete the dialogue about weekend plans.

lines.speaker: Eva
lines.text: V sobotu ___ (I'm going to the cinema) a v neděli ___ (I'm playing tennis).

lines.speaker: Tomáš
lines.text: Super! ___ (I like tennis too). Můžu jít s tebou?

data.explanation: o víkendu = this weekend / on the weekend. jdu do kina = I'm going to the cinema. Hraju tenis = I'm playing tennis (present for near future). Taky rád hraju tenis = I like playing tennis too.

exercises.answer_key: o víkendu jdu do kina hraju tenis Taky rád hraju tenis

exercises.prompt: Arrange to form: 'On Sundays I always go for a walk.'

data.explanation: V neděli = On Sunday(s). vždycky = always. chodím na procházku = I go for a walk. Czech word order: time expression first, then frequency, then verb.

exercises.answer_key: V neděli vždycky chodím na procházku.

exercises.prompt: Translate 'My hobbies are reading, photography, and playing guitar' to Czech.

data.source: My hobbies are reading, photography, and playing guitar
accepted_answers: Moje koníčky jsou čtení, focení a hra na kytaru
Mé koníčky jsou čtení, fotografování a hraní na kytaru
Moje koníčky jsou čtení, fotografování a hra na kytaru
data.grammar_note: Moje/Mé koníčky = my hobbies. čtení = reading, focení/fotografování = photography, hra na kytaru / hraní na kytaru = playing guitar. Note how verb-based nouns end in -ní/-tí.

exercises.answer_key: Moje koníčky jsou čtení, focení a hra na kytaru

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.text: A ___ (do you play) na nějaký nástroj?

data.explanation: Jaké máš koníčky? = What hobbies do you have? Mám rád sport = I like sports. hraješ = do you play (2nd person). hraju na kytaru = I play guitar. nástroj = instrument.

exercises.answer_key: koníčky Mám rád sport fotbal někdy hraješ hraju na kytaru
